english: daṅ bud-méd (dang bud med), men and women B. & C.; emphatically: rgyál-po gčig-po skyés-pa yin (rgyal po gcig po skyes pa yin) the king alone is a man Dzl.; husband Glr.; = skyés-bu (skyes bu) a holy man?

Ssk. पुरुष man, people; skyés-bu gaṅ (skyes bu gang) whosoever; man opp. to the rest of nature Med.; one (French: on), skyés-bu lág-pa brkyáṅ-ba tsám-gyis (skyes bu lag pa brkyang ba tsam gyis) as quick as one stretches out his hand Dzl. - Though this word may also be applied to culprits and criminals (Pth.), it is chiefly used of holy men: skyés-bu dám-pa (skyes bu dam pa) the saint; dad-ldán sky. (dad ldan sky.) the believing, the faithful Glr.; skyés-bu čén-po (skyes bu chen po), महापुरुष the great saint, in Buddhistic writings nearly identical with Buddha; skyés-bu mčog (skyes bu mchog) id. (For the 32 chief characteristics and the 80 subordinate marks distinguishing such a person refer to Köppen. I. 433. Burn. II. 553 ff. Gyatch. c. VII.)
1. fem. of skyés-pa (skyes pa), she that has been born Mil. - 2. fern, = skyás-ma (skyas ma) Sik.

1. vb. to be weary, ccir: bdag kyim-gyis skyó-ste (bdag khyim gyis skyo ste) I being weary of living in the world Dzl.; in a more general sense: to be ill-humoured, grieved, vexed, to feel an aversion Tar. 12. 13; skyo mi śés-par (skyo mi shes par) or skyo mi śés-pa tsám-du (skyo mi shes pa tsam du) without being tired, indefatigably; nam skyo-na (nam skyo na) when he was tired of it Dzl. - 2. sbst. weariness tsol-tsól-nas skyó-ba yaṅ skyé-bar dug (tsol tsol nas skyo ba yang skye bar dug) we are quite tired of that constant seeking Mil.; yid yóṅs-su mi skyó-ba (yid yongs su mi skyo ba) indefatigableness, perseverance Thgy. - skyó-mo (skyo mo) adj., *sems skyó-mo rag* (sems skyo mo rag) I feel discontented, disheartened Ld. Comp. skyo-grógs (skyo grogs) comforter, companion Glr., Mil. - skyo-glú (skyo glu) Cs.: a mournful song. - skyo-ṅál (skyo ngal), skyo-dúb (skyo dub) weariness, skyo-ṅal-méd-pai dád-pa (skyo ngal med pai dad pa) unwearied faith Mil. - skyo-śás (skyo shas) disgust, aversion. - skyo-sáṅs (skyo sangs) recreation, skyo-sáṅs-la °gró-ba (skyo sangs la 'gro ba), resp. °byon-pa ('byon pa) to take a walk or a ride, to promenade. - skyo-bsún-pa (skyo bsun pa) to be grieved Sch.

1. pap of parched meal and beer; any pap, paste or dough; skyó-ma °byúg-pa (skyo ma 'byug pa) to spread paste (upon a wound, as a salve) Med.; śa-skyó (sha skyo) Med.? (it may denote a paste of meat as well as one of mushrooms). - 2. blame, slander, skyó-ma máṅ-la (skyo ma mang la) when he slanders a great deal Mil.

1. scoop, ladle. - *me-kyóg* (me kyog) coal-shovel C.; *źu-kyóg* (zhu kyog) melting-spoon, crucible C. W. - 3. drinking-cup, bowl, goblet. - gser-skyógs (gser skyogs), dṅul-skyógs (dngul skyogs) gold, silver goblet. źal-skyógs (zhal skyogs) C. B., *don-skyógs* (don skyogs) W. resp.: drinking-cup. krag-skyógs (khrag skyogs) bowl for drinking blood, a skull used for that purpose Pth.; *kyog-źáb sal* (kyog zhabs sal) may I ask your honour for the foot of your cup (viz the remnant of your drink)? W. - 3. srab-skyógs (srab skyogs) Cs.: the rein of a bridle. -
to turn, mgrin-pa (mgrin pa) the neck, = to look round, back, Mil., also = to turn away, aside C.

pf. bskyaṅs (bskyangs), ft. bskyaṅ (bskyang), imp. (b)skyoṅ(s) ((b)skyong(s)) Ssk. पा, रक्ष to guard; to keep, to tend, cattle; to defend, the religion; to save, preserve, the life, the body; support, to take care of, poor people, e.g. drin bzáṅ-pos (drin bzang pos) by benefits, favours, thabs-kyis (thabs kyis) by various means; to attend to; to be given to, thugs-dám (thugs dam) meditation, lag-lén (lag len) exercise; rgyal-sríd skyóṅ-ba (rgyal srid skyong ba) to rule, govern a kingdom, čos bźin-du (chos bzhin du) in conformity with the law of religion, justly. - čos-skyóṅ (chos skyong) protector, defender of religion, धर्मपाल, is used for a certain individual deity, or = °jig-rten-skyóṅ ('jig rten skyong), or for a class of magicians in the monasteries of C., v. Schl. 157. Kö. II. 259. - °jig-rten-skyóṅ ('jig rten skyong), लोकपाल 'guardian of the world'; there are four of them, identical with rgyal-čén bźi (rgyal chen bzhi) the four great spirit-kings, q.v. - skyoṅ-dál (skyong dal) assistance C., *kyoṅ-dhál jhé'-pa* (kyong dhal jhe' pa) to help. - skyoṅ-ma (skyong ma) = brtán-ma (brtan ma) the goddess of the earth.
pf. & ft. bskyod (bskyod), Ssk. चल्. 1. to move, to agitate, rluṅ-gis yál-ga (rlung gis yal ga) <
